Cedar wood columns wrapped in hammered bronze sheeting, inscribed with cuneiform dedications to Marduk in raised lettering.
Limestone relief showing Assyrian lion hunt, carved in graduated layers with copper chisels, traces of red ochre remain.
Papyrus-bundle columns in sandstone, capitals painted with malachite green, bases wrapped in gold leaf over gesso.
Glazed brick walls forming striding bulls in lapis-blue and ochre-yellow, each brick molded and fired individually.
Alabaster lustral basin with carved rosette interior, corner spouts shaped as bronze lion heads with crystal eyes.
Painted plaster ceiling showing yellow stars on Egyptian blue background, pigments mixed with egg-white binder.
Palm-capital column in limestone, fronds spreading overhead, painted with yellow ochre and outlined in lampblack.
Door lintel carved with winged sun disk in basalt, inlaid with carnelian and lapis lazuli paste.
Floor of fired clay cones pressed into mud-mortar, creating zigzag patterns in black, red, and white.
Bronze-banded cedar doors with repoussé rosettes, hinges mounted on stone pivot-sockets filled with olive oil.
Procession frieze in limestone showing tribute bearers, details enhanced with gold leaf and turquoise inlay.
Clerestory slots beneath cedar roof beams, admitting narrow light shafts onto polished alabaster wall panels below.
Lotus-bud capitals carved in sandstone, petals tightly closed, painted with azurite blue and outlined in dark resin.
Wall panels of mud-brick faced with shell-inlaid geometric mosaics, set in bitumen mortar, mother-of-pearl highlights throughout.
Carved limestone water channel with bull-headed spouts, interior polished smooth, exterior showing ritual purification scenes in low relief.
Wooden pillars of Lebanese cedar on limestone bases, surfaces carved with intertwined serpent motifs, rubbed with beeswax.
Glazed terracotta tiles forming palmette friezes in green and yellow, each tile hand-molded, edges fitted without mortar.
Bronze guardian lion statues flanking threshold, eyes inlaid with white limestone and obsidian, tongues of red jasper.
Gypsum wall panels carved with winged genies holding pine cones, bodies traced with gold leaf over red ochre.
Roof beams of tamarisk wood resting on mud-brick corbels, undersides painted with repeating chevron patterns in mineral pigments.
Basalt threshold slab carved with cuneiform boundary curse, surface worn smooth by centuries of sandaled feet and oil libations.
Interior walls coated with lime plaster, painted with processional dancers in hematite red and yellow ochre, carbon-black outlines.
Stone drain spouts carved as crouching frog figures, mouths open, bodies stippled with bronze chisels to suggest warty skin.
Courtyard pavement of limestone slabs with carved drainage grooves, corners marked by conical stones painted with protective eye symbols.
Faience tile inlay depicting sacred tree motif, turquoise-glazed ceramic pieces set in bitumen, flanked by kneeling worshippers in white.
